name: Multiobjective 0-1 Knapsack
description: Select a subset of items with weights and two types of values (e.g., profit and risk) to optimize multiple criteria under a single knapsack capacity constraint.

variables.description: 1 if item i is selected, 0 otherwise

objective.expression: Two objectives, e.g. Σᵢ p_i x_i (profit) and Σᵢ q_i x_i (secondary value).

constraints.expression: Σᵢ w_i x_i ≤ W
constraints.description: Total weight must not exceed capacity W

formulation_latex: \max \left( \sum_i p_i x_i, \; \sum_i q_i x_i \right) \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_i w_i x_i \le W,\quad x_i \in \{0,1\}.

name: Maximum Multicommodity Flow
description: Given a directed network with arc capacities and multiple commodities, each with a source, sink, and demand, route flow for all commodities so that arc capacities are respected and the total flow of all commodities is maximized.

variables.description: flow of commodity k on arc (i,j)
variables.domain: x_{ij}^k ≥ 0 for each commodity k and arc (i,j)

objective.expression: Total flow of all commodities: Σ_k Σ_{(i,j)} x_{ij}^k

constraints.expression: For each commodity k and node i: Σ_{(i,j)} x_{ij}^k − Σ_{(j,i)} x_{ji}^k = b_i^k
constraints.description: Flow conservation for each commodity at every node (with supplies/demands encoded in b_i^k)

constraints.expression: Σ_k x_{ij}^k ≤ u_{ij} for each arc (i,j)
constraints.description: Total flow of all commodities on an arc cannot exceed its capacity u_{ij}

formulation_latex: \max \sum_{k \in K} \sum_{(i,j) \in A} x_{ij}^k \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_{(i,j) \in A} x_{ij}^k - \sum_{(j,i) \in A} x_{ji}^k = b_i^k \; \forall i \in V, k \in K, \quad \sum_{k \in K} x_{ij}^k \le u_{ij} \; \forall (i,j) \in A, \quad x_{ij}^k \ge 0.
source: classic multicommodity flow formulations in the literature

name: Steiner Tree in Graphs
description: Given an undirected graph with edge costs and a subset of required terminals, find a minimum-cost tree that connects all terminals (possibly using extra Steiner nodes).

variables.description: 1 if edge e is selected in the Steiner tree, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: For every cut (S, V \ S) with at least one terminal on each side: Σ_{e ∈ δ(S)} x_e ≥ 1
constraints.description: Every terminal-separated cut must contain at least one selected edge to keep terminals connected

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{e \in E} c_e x_e \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_{e \in \delta(S)} x_e \ge 1 \; \forall S \subset V \text{ with terminals on both sides}, \quad x_e \in \{0,1\}.
source: standard Steiner tree formulations in combinatorial optimization textbooks

name: Minimum Cost Circulation
description: Given a directed network with lower and upper bounds and costs on arcs, find a feasible circulation that satisfies flow conservation at every node and minimizes total cost.

variables.description: flow on arc (i,j)
variables.domain: ℓ_{ij} ≤ x_{ij} ≤ u_{ij} for each arc (i,j)

constraints.expression: For each node i: Σ_{(i,j) ∈ A} x_{ij} − Σ_{(j,i) ∈ A} x_{ji} = 0

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{(i,j) \in A} c_{ij} x_{ij} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_{(i,j) \in A} x_{ij} - \sum_{(j,i) \in A} x_{ji} = 0 \; \forall i \in V, \quad \ell_{ij} \le x_{ij} \le u_{ij} \; \forall (i,j) \in A.
complexity: P (with polynomial-time combinatorial algorithms); integer variants may be harder
source: standard min-cost circulation formulations in combinatorial optimization textbooks

name: Maximum Bipartite Matching (ILP)
description: Given a bipartite graph with parts U and V, select a maximum-size set of edges so that no two selected edges share an endpoint.

variables.description: 1 if edge (u,v) is in the matching, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: For each u ∈ U: Σ_{v : (u,v) ∈ E} x_{uv} ≤ 1
constraints.description: Each vertex u in U is incident to at most one selected edge

constraints.expression: For each v ∈ V: Σ_{u : (u,v) ∈ E} x_{uv} ≤ 1
constraints.description: Each vertex v in V is incident to at most one selected edge

formulation_latex: \max \sum_{(u,v) \in E} x_{uv} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_{v : (u,v) \in E} x_{uv} \le 1 \; \forall u \in U, \quad \sum_{u : (u,v) \in E} x_{uv} \le 1 \; \forall v \in V, \quad x_{uv} \in \{0,1\}.
complexity: P (polynomial-time algorithms such as Hungarian or augmenting paths)
source: standard bipartite matching formulations in combinatorial optimization textbooks

name: Hamiltonian Path Problem
description: Given a graph, find a path that visits each vertex exactly once, or determine that no such path exists. The optimization variant seeks a minimum-cost such path when edge costs are given.

variables.description: 1 if edge (i,j) is on the path, 0 otherwise
1 if vertex v is at position i in the path, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: Σ_v π_iv = 1 for each position i
constraints.description: Each position has exactly one vertex

constraints.expression: Σ_i π_iv = 1 for each vertex v
Adjacent positions must be connected by an edge

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{i,j} c_{ij} x_{ij} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{each vertex once, path connectivity},\quad x_{ij},\pi_{iv} \in \{0,1\}.

name: Chinese Postman Problem
description: Given a connected undirected graph with edge weights, find a shortest closed walk that traverses every edge at least once. Equivalent to minimizing total cost of duplicating edges so the graph becomes Eulerian.

variables.description: number of times edge e is traversed (or duplicated)
variables.domain: x_e ≥ 1 integer for each edge e

constraints.expression: x_e ≥ 1 for each edge e
constraints.description: Each edge covered at least once

constraints.expression: For each vertex v: sum of x_e over edges incident to v is even

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{e \in E} w_e x_e \quad \text{s.t.} \quad x_e \ge 1 \; \forall e, \quad \text{even degree } \forall v.
complexity: P (reduces to matching on odd-degree vertices)

name: Hitting Set Problem
description: Given a universe of elements with costs and a family of subsets, choose a minimum-cost set of elements such that every subset in the family is hit (contains at least one chosen element). Dual of set cover.

variables.description: 1 if element e is chosen, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: Σ_{e ∈ S} x_e ≥ 1 for each subset S in the family
constraints.description: Each set must be hit by at least one chosen element

formulation_latex: \min \sum_e c_e x_e \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_{e \in S} x_e \ge 1 \; \forall S, \quad x_e \in \{0,1\}.

name: Cutting Stock Problem
description: Given stock pieces of length L and demands for smaller items of given lengths, determine how to cut the minimum number of stock pieces to satisfy demand. Often formulated via column generation (patterns).

variables.description: number of times cutting pattern p is used
number of items of type i in pattern p

objective.expression: Σ_p x_p (number of stock pieces used)

constraints.expression: Σ_p a_ip x_p ≥ d_i for each item type i
constraints.description: Demand d_i for each item must be met

constraints.expression: Σ_i ℓ_i a_ip ≤ L for each pattern p
constraints.description: Pattern fits in stock length L

formulation_latex: \min \sum_p x_p \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_p a_{ip} x_p \ge d_i \; \forall i, \quad x_p \in \mathbb{Z}_+.

name: Capacitated Minimum Spanning Tree
description: Given a graph with edge costs, a root node, and vertex demands, find a minimum-cost spanning tree rooted at the root such that the total demand in any subtree (connected component when root is removed) does not exceed a given capacity Q.

variables.description: 1 if edge e is in the tree, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: Spanning tree: n-1 edges, connectivity
For every subtree not containing root: Σ_{v in subtree} d_v ≤ Q

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{e \in E} c_e x_e \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{spanning tree}, \quad \text{subtree demand } \le Q.

name: Degree-Constrained Minimum Spanning Tree
description: Given a graph with edge costs and an upper bound B on vertex degree, find a minimum-cost spanning tree in which every vertex has degree at most B.

variables.description: 1 if edge e is in the tree, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: Σ_e x_e = n − 1
For each vertex v: Σ_{e ∋ v} x_e ≤ B
Subtour elimination or connectivity

formulation_latex: \min \sum_e c_e x_e \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_e x_e = n-1, \; \sum_{e \ni v} x_e \le B \; \forall v, \quad x_e \in \{0,1\}.

name: Clique Cover Problem
description: Given an undirected graph, partition the vertices into a minimum number of cliques (sets of pairwise adjacent vertices). Equivalently, cover all vertices with the fewest cliques.

variables.description: 1 if clique k is used, 0 otherwise
1 if vertex v is in clique k, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: Σ_k x_vk = 1 for each vertex v
constraints.description: Each vertex in exactly one clique

constraints.expression: For each clique k: if x_uk = x_vk = 1 and (u,v) not an edge then y_k = 0

formulation_latex: \min \sum_k y_k \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_k x_{vk}=1 \; \forall v, \quad \text{clique constraints}, \quad x_{vk}, y_k \in \{0,1\}.

name: Minimum Spanning Tree
description: Given a connected undirected graph with edge costs, find a spanning tree of minimum total edge cost. A tree that connects all vertices with minimum sum of edge weights.

variables.description: 1 if edge e is in the tree, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: Σ_e x_e = n − 1
Σ_{e ∈ δ(S)} x_e ≥ 1 for each proper subset S of vertices

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{e \in E} c_e x_e \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_e x_e = n-1, \; \sum_{e \in \delta(S)} x_e \ge 1 \; \forall \emptyset \subset S \subset V, \quad x_e \in \{0,1\}.

name: Minimum Bisection
description: Partition the vertices of a graph into two sets of equal (or nearly equal) size so that the number (or weight) of edges between the two sets is minimized.

variables.description: 1 if vertex v is in one side, 0 for the other
1 if edge (u,v) is in the cut

constraints.expression: Σ_v x_v = n/2 (or floor/ceil)
y_uv ≥ x_u − x_v and y_uv ≥ x_v − x_u
constraints.description: Edge in cut iff endpoints on different sides

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{(u,v) \in E} w_{uv} y_{uv} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_v x_v = n/2, \quad y_{uv} \ge |x_u - x_v|, \quad x_v, y_{uv} \in \{0,1\}.

name: Prize-Collecting Steiner Tree
description: Given a graph with edge costs and vertex prizes (or penalties for not including), find a subtree that minimizes total edge cost plus the sum of penalties of vertices not in the subtree (or maximizes profit of included vertices minus edge cost).

variables.description: 1 if edge e is in the tree, 0 otherwise
1 if vertex v is in the subtree, 0 otherwise

objective.expression: Σ_e c_e x_e + Σ_v π_v (1 − z_v) (edge cost plus penalties for excluded vertices)

constraints.expression: Selected edges form a tree; z_v = 1 if v is in the tree
constraints.description: Tree connectivity and linking z to edges

formulation_latex: \min \sum_e c_e x_e + \sum_v \pi_v(1-z_v) \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{tree}, \quad x_e, z_v \in \{0,1\}.

name: Single Machine Weighted Completion Time
description: Schedule n jobs on a single machine; each job has a processing time and a weight. Minimize the total weighted completion time. Solvable in polynomial time by Smith's rule (WSPT).

variables.description: completion time of job j
1 if job j is in position k

constraints.expression: Σ_k x_jk = 1 for each j, Σ_j x_jk = 1 for each k
constraints.description: Each job in one position, each position one job

constraints.expression: C_j = Σ_k (Σ_{ℓ≤k} p_{[ℓ]}) x_jk (completion time by position)

formulation_latex: \min \sum_j w_j C_j \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{permutation of jobs},\quad C_j \text{ completion time}.

name: Parallel Machine Makespan
description: Schedule n jobs on m identical parallel machines; each job has a processing time. Minimize the makespan (maximum completion time over all machines).

variables.description: 1 if job j is assigned to machine i
makespan

constraints.expression: Σ_i x_ij = 1 for each job j
constraints.description: Each job assigned to one machine

constraints.expression: Σ_j p_j x_ij ≤ C_max for each machine i
constraints.description: Machine load does not exceed makespan

formulation_latex: \min C_{\max} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_i x_{ij}=1 \; \forall j, \; \sum_j p_j x_{ij} \le C_{\max} \; \forall i, \quad x_{ij} \in \{0,1\}.

name: Weighted Set Cover
description: Given a universe, a family of subsets each with a cost, choose a collection of subsets of minimum total cost that covers every element of the universe (each element in at least one chosen set).

variables.description: 1 if subset S is chosen, 0 otherwise

constraints.expression: Σ_{S : e ∈ S} x_S ≥ 1 for each element e

formulation_latex: \min \sum_S c_S x_S \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_{S : e \in S} x_S \ge 1 \; \forall e, \quad x_S \in \{0,1\}.

name: Maximum Acyclic Subgraph
description: Given a directed graph with edge weights, find a maximum-weight subset of edges that forms an acyclic subgraph. Equivalently, order the vertices and keep only edges going forward; maximize total weight of such edges.

variables.description: position (rank) of vertex v in the ordering
1 if edge (u,v) is in the subgraph (u before v)

constraints.expression: y_uv = 1 iff π_u < π_v
constraints.description: Edge kept only if tail precedes head in ordering

formulation_latex: \max \sum_{(u,v) \in A} w_{uv} y_{uv} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad y_{uv} = 1 \Leftrightarrow \pi_u < \pi_v, \quad \pi \text{ permutation}.

name: Open Vehicle Routing Problem
aliases: routes that do not return to depot
description: Like the capacitated VRP but routes do not return to the depot; each route starts at the depot, visits a subset of customers, and ends at the last customer. Minimize total travel cost.

variables.description: 1 if arc (i,j) is traversed by vehicle k
1 if customer i is served by vehicle k

constraints.expression: Each customer visited exactly once; flow conservation; capacity per vehicle
Route starts at depot, ends at a customer (no return)

formulation_latex: \min \sum_k \sum_{i,j} c_{ij} x_{ij}^k \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{open routes, capacity, visit once}.

name: Capacitated Arc Routing Problem
description: Given a graph with required edges (tasks) with demands and a depot, find a set of vehicle routes that service all required edges (each at least once) subject to vehicle capacity, minimizing total cost. Servicing an edge means traversing it and adding its demand to the load.

variables.description: 1 if arc (i,j) is traversed by vehicle k
1 if required edge e is serviced by vehicle k

constraints.expression: Each required edge serviced exactly once; vehicle capacity; routes start/end at depot

formulation_latex: \min \sum_k \sum_{(i,j)} c_{ij} x_{ij}^k \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{service all required edges, capacity, depot}.

name: Multilevel Lot-Sizing Problem
description: Production system with a bill-of-materials (BOM): items at different levels have demands (external or derived from parent). Decide when to produce how much at each level to minimize setup and holding costs while meeting demand.

variables.description: production quantity of item i in period t
1 if item i is produced in period t (setup)
inventory of item i at end of period t

objective.expression: Σ_i Σ_t (s_it y_it + h_it I_it) (setups + holding)

constraints.expression: I_{i,t-1} + x_it = d_it + Σ_j r_ij x_jt + I_it for each i,t
x_it ≤ M y_it

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{i,t} (s_{it} y_{it} + h_{it} I_{it}) \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{balance, BOM}, \quad x_{it} \le M y_{it}.

name: Team Orienteering Problem
description: Given a set of nodes with scores (prizes), a depot, and a time limit, find a set of routes for a fixed number of vehicles starting and ending at the depot such that each node is visited at most once and total collected score is maximized while each route respects the time limit.

variables.description: 1 if vehicle k travels from i to j
1 if node i is visited by some vehicle

constraints.expression: Flow conservation; each route starts/ends at depot; time limit per route
z_i ≤ 1; z_i = 1 if i visited
constraints.description: At most one visit per node

formulation_latex: \max \sum_i s_i z_i \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \text{routes from depot, time limit}, \quad z_i \in \{0,1\}.

name: Minimum Feedback Arc Set
description: Given a directed graph with arc weights, find a minimum-weight set of arcs whose removal makes the graph acyclic. Equivalent to ordering vertices so that the total weight of backward edges is minimized.

variables.description: position of vertex v in linear ordering
1 if (u,v) is a backward edge (removed)

constraints.expression: y_uv = 1 iff π_v < π_u (v before u)
constraints.description: Backward edges are those going from higher to lower rank

formulation_latex: \min \sum_{(u,v)} w_{uv} y_{uv} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \pi \text{ ordering}, \; y_{uv} = 1 \Leftrightarrow \pi_v < \pi_u.

name: Stable Marriage Problem
description: Given n men and n women each with a strict preference order over the other set, find a perfect matching such that no unmatched pair (man, woman) both prefer each other to their current partners. Optimization variants minimize regret or maximize stability.

variables.description: 1 if man i is matched to woman j

objective.expression: e.g. sum of ranks (regret) or number of stable pairs

constraints.expression: Σ_j x_ij = 1 for each i, Σ_i x_ij = 1 for each j
No blocking pair: for no (i,j) do both prefer each other to current match

formulation_latex: \text{Perfect matching} \quad \sum_j x_{ij}=\sum_i x_{ij}=1, \quad \text{no blocking pair}.
complexity: P (Gale–Shapley algorithm); some optimization variants NP-hard
